best_version: Graphics Preset: Low (do this first) Motion Blur: 0% VSync: Off (I didn’t notice any screen tearing on my Steam Deck OLED, and prefer to stay above 30FPS when possible) Ray Tracing: Disabled (This is ON by default and will tank your performance) Upscaling: AMD FidelityFX 3 FSR Quality: Balanced or Performance FSR Sharpness: This is personal preference, but I’d start at 35% and increase it from there. The Advanced Settings that are below “Graphics Quality” should all remain at Low, but you can experiment with changing Texture Quality and Post Processing Quality to improve the appearance of textures, at a very slight hit to frame rate. In the “UI” tab, set text size to Largest Additionally, it may be helpful to go into the game’s Properties before you launch it, and ensure the “Compatibility” setting is checked and “Proton Hotfix” is selected. This will just ensure that if Valve makes any improvements that will benefit Steam Deck players (as it frequently does, most famously with Elden Ring), you’ll be first in line to get them.

best_version: High Concept Premises #premise Critical Factor Bypass: Bypassing the critical factor means moving a subject from their conscious mind into their unconscious mind. The critical factor is an internal filter that checks what is allowed in and what is not. It acts as a filter between the logical part of the brain, the conscious mind, and the emotional part, the subconscious. Hypnosis is a way to bypass the critical factor. Under hypnosis, the critical filter goes to sleep for a while. The conscious mind stays awake and can still make decisions. The subconscious is receptive to any suggestion that the conscious mind wants to let in. One way to bypass the critical factor is with a shock. To do this, deliver the shock and immediately follow up with a command to go into hypnosis. Neurolinguistic Programming: Neuro-linguistic programming (NLP) is a psychological approach that uses techniques to improve communication, relationships, and personal development. NLP is based on the idea that thoughts, language, and behaviors are connected. By changing one of these elements, you can change the others. NLP involves analyzing strategies used by successful people and applying them to reach a personal goal. It may reduce anxiety and improve overall well-being. NLP has become widespread since it started in the 1970s. However, there is no scientific evidence supporting the claims made by NLP advocates, and it has been called a pseudoscience. Scientific reviews have shown that NLP is based on outdated metaphors of the brain's inner workings that are inconsistent with current neurological theory.

best_version: Comedy Writing Class 25 Jokes #ComedyWriting 1. Family sues Delta after drunken passenger allegedly groped mom and her teen daughter on flight: The passenger was arrested and charged with disorderly conduct and incest, for groping his mom and teen sister. 2. Tennessee has placed a 6% tax on cannabis derived THC products, a controversial decision considering Marijuana is not legal in the state: For those who don't know, THC is an acronym which means "Tennessee Hates Cannabis" *3. U.S. ban on popular lightbulb goes into effect Aug. 1: U.S. residents will no longer be able to buy blacklights without a medical marijuana card. *4. 1st US nuclear reactor built from scratch in decades enters commercial operation in Georgia: I'm sorry, that's a typo... 1st US new color tractor built from scratch in decades enters commercial operation in Georgia. They're pink. 5. Chinese zoo denies its sun bears are humans dressed in costumes: They issued a statement, saying, "That was just zhou (Joe) He was weird. He's in prison now. Please come back." Roughly translated. 6. S.C. judge orders Woman accused of killing bride in DUI golf cart crash must remain in custody: The real crime, though, is having your wedding on a golf course. • 7. Vegan influencer Zhanna Samsonova 'dies from starvation and exhaustion' after switching to diet of tropical fruit: But you know what she didn't die of? Scurvy. Tropical Fruits up One. 8. Fatal stabbing of NYC gay man is being investigated as a possible hate crime: The accused claims they did not know the victim was gay, stating, "There is no hate in my heart. I just be stabbin' people sometimes. " *9. NASA hears signal from Voyager 2 spacecraft after mistakenly cutting contact: The message, according to scientists, was, "Sorry I lost you, I was going through a tunnel." 10.Former Lizzo dancers were weight-shamed and pressured while at strip club, lawsuit says: The former dancers have been identified as Lizzo's Ankles. 11.Arkansas deputy shoots at Pomeranian but hits woman standing on porch instead: Thank God! For a second I thought this was gonna be one of those sad "Cop shoots Dog" stories. 12.Appeals court throws out conviction after judge says Black man 'looks like a criminal to me': The judge elaborated on his statement saying, "He was wearing an orange prison jumpsuit, had an inmate number sewn on his chest, and was black." Dress for the job you want, not the one you have, right? 13. 7-Eleven workers beat would-be robber with stick until suspect starts crying: The suspect was not stealing anything, but it was Arkansas, and he was black. 14. Tickets for feeding Houston's homeless thrown out after police no-shows: In other news, Authorities are investigating homicide involving a police officer who was fed to the homeless. 15. Two people caught swine flu after visiting pig exhibits at agricultural fairs: Fair Organizers have placed new regulations on exhibits, now requiring all pigs get vaccinated and wear a masks. Some Pigs claimed this violated their civil liberties and stormed the Funnel Cake Truck. One Pig died on the scene and two are in critical condition. No funnel cakes were injured. 16. Mark Margolis, actor known for roles in 'Breaking Bad' and 'Scarface,' dies at 83: Was she the one that played Walter's wife? No? Dang! I was hoping it was her. 17: Montana otter attack leaves 3 women hurt one sent hospital: Montana Wildlife Control are still searching for the adorable attackers. 18: Florida effectively bans AP Psychology course over LGBTQ content, College Board says: The Board's main concern was that since there is already an L a G and a B Why do they need the Q?
